title: schoenoplectus supinus subsp. hallii
intro: Schoenoplectus supinus subsp. hallii, also known as Hall's bulrush, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the sedge family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing swelling, relieving pain, and aiding in the treatment of urinary tract infections and kidney stones. Hall's bulrush can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for internal use, and its rhizomes and roots can be used in traditional medicine preparations.
